description_long: This is a medium-sized helicopter with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily in red and white, featuring Coast Guard markings and the number 276 on its side. The helicopter has a prominent main rotor with long, slender blades and a smaller tail rotor at the end of its tail boom. The cockpit is equipped with large, angular windows that provide wide visibility for the pilots, and the doors and side panels are clearly defined. The landing gear consists of sturdy black skids with crossbars for stability during ground contact. The tail section is painted red and includes a horizontal stabilizer and a vertical fin, both with visible markings. The overall design suggests a utility or rescue helicopter, suitable for urban or coastal operations.
description: A red and white Coast Guard helicopter with large cockpit windows, long rotor blades, and black landing skids, marked with the number 276.
description_view_0: Side view showing the red and white fuselage, large cockpit windows, black landing skids, and the number 276 clearly visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide cockpit windows, the main rotor blade extending horizontally, and the narrow profile of the skids.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the Coast Guard markings, the tail boom, and the full length of the main rotor blade.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail rotor, the red tail fin, and the symmetrical arrangement of the landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: The main rotor has two long, narrow blades made of composite or metal, colored gray with no visible markings, each blade extending well beyond the width of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: The tail rotor is present on the right side of the tail boom, featuring two short blades oriented vertically, colored gray with a red hub, and no visible protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: The helicopter has two black, tubular landing skids with crossbars for stability, made of metal, and attached to the underside of the fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_windows: There are four large, angular cockpit windows at the front, arranged in a wraparound style, with black framing and a slight tint, providing wide visibility.
